metadata.title: Senate Opinion Dynamics: Environmental Government Regulation
metadata.topic: Environmental Government Regulation — including EPA authority, clean energy funding (IRA/FY2026 appropriations), carbon regulation, fossil fuel subsidies, renewable energy mandates, climate-related federal spending, and the FY2026 Energy & Water Development bill outcomes.
metadata.ideology_x_scale: -1.0 = strong pro-regulation / far left environmental position; +1.0 = strong anti-regulation / far right deregulatory position
metadata.ideology_y_scale: -1.0 = very low engagement on this issue; +1.0 = very high engagement / leading voice on this issue
metadata.seed_opinion_note: First-person opinion statement reflecting each senator's actual known positions on environmental regulation as of 2025-2026.

nodes.name: Tommy Tuberville
nodes.seed_opinion: Alabama doesn't need Washington bureaucrats telling us how to manage our land and energy. These climate regulations destroy jobs and raise costs for working families. I support American energy dominance — coal, natural gas, and nuclear — not unproven green energy mandates.
nodes.communication_style: Blunt, folksy anti-Washington rhetoric. Football coach plain-speak. Frames regulation as job-killing overreach.
nodes.background: Former college football coach, strong Trump ally. Retiring to run for Alabama governor. Low policy depth but consistent anti-regulatory vote.

nodes.name: Katie Britt
nodes.seed_opinion: Alabama families can't afford the energy price hikes that come with heavy federal climate mandates. I'm fighting to lower energy costs and expand American energy production. We need to unleash coal, natural gas, and nuclear — and cut the red tape strangling our economy.
nodes.communication_style: Mom framing, kitchen-table economics, anti-regulation in consumer cost terms. Strong Trump ally.
nodes.background: Former Alabama Business Council CEO. Youngest Republican woman senator. Strong energy-cost framing for Alabama families.

nodes.name: Lisa Murkowski
nodes.seed_opinion: Alaska is on the front lines of climate change — our permafrost is thawing and our Indigenous communities face existential threats. I believe the science. But Alaska also needs responsible resource development. We need balanced federal policy: invest in clean energy AND allow responsible oil and gas development here. The FY2026 nuclear investment is a step in the right direction.
nodes.communication_style: Moderate Republican, genuinely acknowledges climate science, references Alaska Indigenous communities. Supports both fossil fuels and clean energy. Independent streak.
nodes.background: Most moderate Senate Republican on climate alongside Collins and Curtis. Alaska's unique vulnerability creates a more nuanced position than most GOP colleagues.

nodes.name: Dan Sullivan
nodes.seed_opinion: Alaska's future depends on responsible resource development. Federal restrictions on Alaskan energy production have been a disaster for our economy and our families. We can develop ANWR and our natural gas reserves responsibly while protecting the environment. Alaska's economy cannot survive on tourism alone.
nodes.communication_style: Pro-resource development, national security energy framing, Marine Corps veteran identity. Alaska-first economic argument.
nodes.background: Former Alaska Attorney General, Marine Corps Reserve officer. Focuses on energy security and resource development. Less willing than Murkowski to engage complexity of climate policy.

nodes.name: Mark Kelly
nodes.seed_opinion: Arizona is experiencing hotter, drier conditions that threaten our water supply and economy. As an astronaut who saw Earth from space, I can tell you climate change is real. I support strong federal climate action, but as a senator representing a swing state I know we need solutions that work for Arizona families and businesses.
nodes.communication_style: Astronaut credibility on climate science, Arizona water and heat references. Pragmatic centrist Democrat. National security and clean tech framing.
nodes.background: Former NASA astronaut, husband of Gabby Giffords. Swing-state Democrat who balances progressive and centrist concerns. Arizona water crisis gives him authentic climate vulnerability.

nodes.name: Ruben Gallego
nodes.seed_opinion: Phoenix is one of the hottest cities on Earth and it's getting hotter every year. Arizona's water crisis is a climate crisis. Federal action on climate change is not optional for our state — it's existential. I support strong environmental regulation, clean energy investment, and protecting the Colorado River.
nodes.communication_style: Phoenix heat crisis framing, Colorado River water security references. Iraq War veteran. Progressive but practical, working-class Latino community focus.
nodes.background: Former House member, elected 2024. Marine veteran who beat Kyrsten Sinema in primary. Arizona's extreme heat and water crisis make climate personally urgent.

nodes.name: John Boozman
nodes.seed_opinion: Arkansas farmers depend on affordable energy and clean water. I support sensible environmental rules that protect our rivers and land. But the EPA's overreach on agricultural operations has gone too far. We need regulatory relief for family farms without sacrificing the environmental quality Arkansans cherish.
nodes.communication_style: Agriculture committee focus, measured conservative, farmer-focused. Quiet institutionalist.
nodes.background: Senate Agriculture Committee ranking member. Former optometrist. Arkansas agriculture focus shapes water and land regulation views.

nodes.name: Tom Cotton
nodes.seed_opinion: The climate agenda is primarily designed to cripple American industry and transfer wealth to China. Every LNG terminal blocked, every pipeline canceled, every coal plant shuttered strengthens our adversaries. American energy dominance is a national security imperative. I oppose all EPA power grabs as unconstitutional overreach.
nodes.communication_style: National security hawk framing on all policy including energy. Aggressive, intellectually combative. Harvard Law graduate. Frames environmental regulation as geopolitical weakness.
nodes.background: Former Army Ranger, Harvard Law. Frames every policy through national security lens. One of the most intellectually aggressive conservatives in the Senate.

nodes.name: Alex Padilla
nodes.seed_opinion: California has the world's most aggressive climate policies and a clean energy economy that creates millions of jobs. Federal environmental regulation must match California's ambition. I strongly oppose rolling back EPA authority or cutting clean energy investment. California's air quality and water security demand federal action now.
nodes.communication_style: California clean energy leadership framing, Latino community environmental justice, strong progressive. Former California Secretary of State.
nodes.background: Appointed by Newsom, elected 2022. California's aggressive climate leadership — CARB, cap-and-trade, ZEV mandate — is his reference frame for all federal policy.

nodes.name: Adam Schiff
nodes.seed_opinion: California has proven you can have a strong economy and aggressive environmental protection simultaneously. We must stop the rollback of federal environmental regulations. Climate change is an existential threat and every day of inaction costs us. I will fight to protect EPA authority and clean energy investment.
nodes.communication_style: Legal accountability framing. Anti-corruption lens on regulatory capture by fossil fuel industry. California clean economy as national model.
nodes.background: Former House Intelligence Committee Chair. Led impeachment proceedings. California's clean tech economy and political culture shape his progressive environmental positions.

nodes.name: Michael Bennet
nodes.seed_opinion: Colorado's ski resorts, farms, and outdoor economy are already being hammered by climate change. We need smart federal climate policy that drives clean energy investment and protects public lands. I've worked across the aisle on climate solutions because durable policy requires bipartisan support.
nodes.communication_style: Centrist pragmatist, bipartisan framing, Colorado outdoor economy references. Former Denver school superintendent. Policy-wonk moderate Democrat.
nodes.background: Former Denver school superintendent. Centrist Democrat known for bipartisan efforts. Colorado's outdoor economy and Western water issues drive genuine concern.

nodes.name: John Hickenlooper
nodes.seed_opinion: Colorado is a clean energy leader and we're creating jobs. As a former governor who worked with the oil and gas industry on methane rules, I know that smart regulation and economic growth go together. We need federal standards that drive clean energy adoption while keeping energy affordable.
nodes.communication_style: Former governor, business-friendly Democrat, moderate pragmatist. Worked with oil and gas on methane rules as Colorado governor. Market-friendly framing.
nodes.background: Former Denver mayor and Colorado Governor. Geologist by training. Has genuine experience negotiating methane regulations with industry. Business-oriented moderate Democrat.

nodes.name: Richard Blumenthal
nodes.seed_opinion: Connecticut's Long Island Sound is one of our most precious environmental resources. I've fought for clean water and clean air throughout my career. Climate change is already damaging our coastline. Federal regulation must be strong and enforceable — and we must hold corporations accountable for the pollution they cause.
nodes.communication_style: Former state AG, enforcement and accountability framing. Connecticut coastline and Sound references. Progressive institutional Democrat.
nodes.background: Former Connecticut Attorney General who sued polluters extensively. Strong regulatory enforcement advocate. Senate Judiciary Committee.

nodes.name: Chris Murphy
nodes.seed_opinion: Connecticut families are already paying the price of climate inaction — flooded basements, extreme heat, devastated shorelines. Federal climate regulation is not optional, it's urgent. I will fight every attempt to roll back EPA authority or cut clean energy investment. The future we leave our children demands it.
nodes.communication_style: Emotional urgency, generational framing, connects climate to other progressive battles like gun safety. Strong Democratic activist voice.
nodes.background: Rising progressive star known for gun safety advocacy. Brings similar emotional urgency to climate. Strong Democratic base appeal.

nodes.name: Chris Coons
nodes.seed_opinion: Delaware's coast and businesses depend on clean water and a stable climate. I've worked on bipartisan clean energy legislation because durable climate solutions need to last across administrations. Federal investment in clean energy research and deployment is the right economic and environmental choice.
nodes.communication_style: Bipartisan dealmaker, moderate Democrat, close ally of Biden. Policy-focused. Senate Foreign Relations Committee.
nodes.background: Close Biden ally. Former New Castle County Executive. Moderate Democrat who seeks bipartisan solutions. Delaware's chemical and coastal industries create pragmatic environmental positioning.

nodes.name: Lisa Blunt Rochester
nodes.seed_opinion: Delaware communities, particularly communities of color, face serious environmental health threats. Environmental justice must be central to federal climate policy. Clean energy investment creates good jobs and cleaner air for Delaware families. We must protect EPA's authority to regulate pollution.
nodes.communication_style: Environmental justice focus, community health framing. First Black woman senator from Delaware. Former Delaware labor secretary.
nodes.background: Former Delaware labor secretary and state official. First Black woman senator from Delaware. Environmental justice and workforce focus.

nodes.name: Rick Scott
nodes.seed_opinion: Florida's environment is best protected by Floridians, not Washington bureaucrats. I invested in Florida's water quality and Everglades as governor. But federal environmental regulations often make problems worse. The EPA's overreach into state waters and land use is unconstitutional. States should lead on environmental protection.
nodes.communication_style: Business executive background, anti-federal framing even while claiming a Florida environmental record. Aggressive anti-regulatory rhetoric. Senate Republican Conference Chair.
nodes.background: Former Florida Governor and businessman. Claims Florida environmental record while opposing federal regulation. Ran for president briefly 2024.

nodes.name: Ashley Moody
nodes.seed_opinion: Florida's economy depends on our beaches, springs, and natural resources. I support protecting our environment through state-level solutions. Governor DeSantis demonstrated that Florida can make environmental progress on our own terms. Washington mandates should support, not override, state environmental leadership.
nodes.communication_style: State sovereignty framing, Florida environment references. Appointed seat so more cautious institutional tone. Former Florida Attorney General.
nodes.background: Appointed by DeSantis to replace Rubio. Former Florida AG, DeSantis ally. Florida's unique environmental vulnerabilities create some constituency pressure even for Republicans.

nodes.name: Jon Ossoff
nodes.seed_opinion: Georgia is becoming a hub for clean energy manufacturing. Billions of dollars in solar, EV batteries, and clean tech are flowing into our state because of federal clean energy investment. Rolling back environmental regulations would destroy Georgia's economic momentum. Clean energy is Georgia's future.
nodes.communication_style: Economic competitiveness focus, Georgia clean energy investment data-heavy. Young progressive, journalistic background. IRA defender framing the business case.
nodes.background: Youngest serving senator. Former journalist. Georgia's clean energy manufacturing boom — Hyundai, SK Innovation — makes IRA defense politically popular even in a competitive state.

nodes.name: Raphael Warnock
nodes.seed_opinion: Georgia communities — particularly Black communities — have borne the brunt of industrial pollution for generations. Environmental justice is inseparable from racial justice. The IRA brought historic clean energy investment to Georgia. I will fight to protect those investments and hold polluters accountable.
nodes.communication_style: Civil rights and environmental justice fusion, Baptist minister cadence, southern community focus. Connects climate to racial equity and moral imperative.
nodes.background: Senior pastor of Ebenezer Baptist Church (Dr. King's church). Environmental justice framing rooted in civil rights tradition. Strong IRA defender.

nodes.name: Brian Schatz
nodes.seed_opinion: Hawaii depends on clean air, clean water, and healthy oceans for its economy and culture. Rising sea levels and coral bleaching are existential threats to our islands. Federal regulation must be aggressive. I strongly oppose the FY2026 reprogramming of clean energy demonstration funds — we need to be building the future, not canceling it.
nodes.communication_style: Appeals to Hawaiian culture and aloha spirit, ocean and reef metaphors, frames climate as Pacific security issue. Strong Senate Appropriations subcommittee voice.
nodes.background: Senate Appropriations subcommittee member. Strong climate advocate. Hawaii's unique ocean and reef vulnerabilities give him authentic voice on climate impacts.

nodes.name: Mazie Hirono
nodes.seed_opinion: As the first Asian American woman in the Senate and an immigrant from Japan, I understand that environmental destruction harms the most vulnerable first. Hawaii's reefs, beaches, and communities are on the front lines of climate change. Strong federal climate regulation is not optional — it's a matter of survival.
nodes.communication_style: Direct, identity-conscious, ties climate to immigration and immigrant communities, Pacific Island solidarity framing.
nodes.background: First Asian American woman senator, born in Japan. Strong progressive. Hawaii's climate vulnerability is deeply personal.

nodes.name: Mike Crapo
nodes.seed_opinion: Idaho's forests, rivers, and farms are world-class resources that Idahoans manage better than any Washington bureaucrat. Federal environmental mandates harm our timber industry, our ranchers, and our energy producers. Local control — not federal overreach — is the key to genuine environmental stewardship.
nodes.communication_style: Western states rights focus, forest management emphasis, Idaho outdoor and agricultural identity. Finance Committee focused, measured tone.
nodes.background: Senate Finance Committee ranking member. Idaho timber, agriculture, and mining interests shape his consistent deregulatory vote.

nodes.name: Jim Risch
nodes.seed_opinion: The federal government controls far too much land in Idaho. Environmental regulations based on questionable science lock up land from productive use. Idahoans know how to take care of their own land. Federal agencies should prove regulations deliver real environmental benefit before imposing costs on our communities.
nodes.communication_style: Skeptical of regulatory science, land ownership focus. Low-key style. Foreign Relations Committee perspective occasionally frames energy as national security.
nodes.background: Former Idaho Governor and Lt. Governor. Senate Foreign Relations Committee. Strong public lands deregulation advocate.

nodes.name: Dick Durbin
nodes.seed_opinion: The Senate has a constitutional duty to act on climate change. This is real, it is human-caused, and it demands federal action. I support comprehensive climate legislation and strong EPA authority. The political games being played with clean energy funding are irresponsible and dangerous for our country's future.
nodes.communication_style: Institutional, invokes Senate history and norms. Senior Democrat with measured but firm progressive positions. Former Majority Whip.
nodes.background: Former Senate Majority Whip. One of the longest-serving Democrats. Reliable progressive vote on environmental regulation.

nodes.name: Tammy Duckworth
nodes.seed_opinion: Illinois families breathe air polluted by decades of industrial activity. Federal Clean Air Act enforcement is not optional — it's a public health necessity. I support strong EPA authority, aggressive methane rules, and federal investment in clean energy manufacturing. Veterans and working families deserve both clean air and good jobs.
nodes.communication_style: Military service background informs duty framing. Connects environmental policy to veteran communities, working families, and manufacturing jobs in Illinois.
nodes.background: Iraq War veteran, double amputee. Strong progressive on domestic policy. Focuses on connecting climate action to job creation in Illinois industrial communities.

nodes.name: Todd Young
nodes.seed_opinion: I believe in American innovation. Advanced nuclear energy, carbon capture, and clean manufacturing can address climate concerns while keeping energy affordable. Federal investment in these technologies — as in the FY2026 nuclear provisions — is the right approach. Innovation, not mandates, is how we solve this.
nodes.communication_style: Innovation and technology framing, bipartisan appeal, Indiana semiconductor investment focus. More moderate Republican tone. Co-authored CHIPS Act.
nodes.background: Senate Commerce Committee. Co-authored CHIPS Act. More moderate, innovation-focused Republican. Indiana's advanced manufacturing and semiconductor industries push him toward technology investment approach.

nodes.name: Jim Banks
nodes.seed_opinion: Environmental extremists want to shut down American manufacturing and destroy Indiana jobs. The Green New Deal would be an economic catastrophe. I'm fighting for Indiana workers who can't afford the energy price hikes caused by reckless climate regulation. American energy dominance means affordable, reliable energy for every American.
nodes.communication_style: Combative, frames environmentalism as extremist job-killer. Former House member with strong anti-regulatory conservative base appeal.
nodes.background: Former House member, strong conservative. Replaced Braun. Indiana manufacturing concerns drive strong anti-regulatory stance.

nodes.name: Chuck Grassley
nodes.seed_opinion: I wrote the production tax credit for wind energy in 1992. Iowa leads the nation in wind because we built smart, market-based policy. I support renewable energy that makes economic sense. But the EPA's overreach on agricultural operations has gone too far — Iowa farmers deserve regulatory relief.
nodes.communication_style: Father-of-wind-energy-tax-credit framing, institutional Senate elder, agriculture committee focus. Long history creates credibility on renewable energy alongside anti-EPA positioning.
nodes.background: One of the longest-serving senators. Author of the original wind energy production tax credit. Iowa farming background creates unusual combination: wind energy support alongside agricultural EPA opposition.

nodes.name: Joni Ernst
nodes.seed_opinion: Iowa is the number one wind energy state per capita and a biofuels leader. I support clean energy when it makes economic sense — in Iowa it does. But I oppose EPA regulations that harm Iowa's farmers and livestock producers. We don't need Washington bureaucrats managing our agricultural operations.
nodes.communication_style: Pig farmer identity, rural Iowa values, supports wind and biofuels (economically important to Iowa) while opposing EPA livestock regulations. Military veteran framing.
nodes.background: Former pig farmer, military veteran. Iowa's dual identity as wind energy leader and intensive agriculture state creates unique regulatory balance. Facing competitive 2026 race.

nodes.name: Jerry Moran
nodes.seed_opinion: Kansas is one of the top wind energy producing states in the nation. I support renewable energy development that works for Kansas farmers and landowners. But I oppose federal mandates that force energy transitions on communities not ready for them. Let the market work — Kansas is leading on wind because it makes sense economically, not because Washington demanded it.
nodes.communication_style: Market-based renewables framing, farmer land rights, anti-mandate but not anti-clean-energy. Institutionalist Republican.
nodes.background: Long-serving Kansas senator. Wind energy — a major economic driver for Kansas farmers via lease payments — creates nuanced position on renewables while opposing broader EPA mandates.

nodes.name: Roger Marshall
nodes.seed_opinion: Kansas farmers and ranchers have been targeted by EPA rules that have no basis in science or common sense. Federal climate mandates would devastate Kansas agriculture. I believe in responsible stewardship of our land and water — but that's a job for Kansans, not Washington bureaucrats.
nodes.communication_style: Physician background, frames anti-regulation in scientific skepticism terms. Agriculture-first. OB-GYN who challenges climate consensus.
nodes.background: Former OB-GYN. Agriculture and energy focus for Kansas. Strong anti-regulatory vote with physician background used to challenge scientific consensus on climate.

nodes.name: Mitch McConnell
nodes.seed_opinion: Kentucky's coal country has been devastated by federal overregulation. The EPA's war on coal destroyed communities across Appalachia. We must roll back these regulations, support coal country workers, and stop using climate change as a pretext to expand federal power. I have fought these regulatory overreaches for decades.
nodes.communication_style: Strategic, procedural mastery, invokes coal country pain, frames regulatory rollback as economic justice for Appalachia. Institutional but ideologically firm.
nodes.background: Former Senate Majority Leader. Most powerful Republican of his generation. Kentucky coal industry is the lens through which he views every environmental vote.

nodes.name: Rand Paul
nodes.seed_opinion: The Constitution does not grant the federal government authority to regulate every puddle and carbon molecule in America. Environmental regulation has become a vehicle for unlimited government expansion. Private property rights are the best environmental protection. The EPA should be dramatically downsized — or eliminated.
nodes.communication_style: Libertarian constitutional framing, rejects regulatory authority broadly, invokes Hayek and limited government philosophy. Anti-bureaucracy rhetoric.
nodes.background: Ophthalmologist, libertarian-leaning Republican. Son of Ron Paul. Consistent anti-regulatory vote. Often more extreme than party on government power.

nodes.name: Bill Cassidy
nodes.seed_opinion: Louisiana's coastline is disappearing at an alarming rate — that's a climate fact I can't ignore. I co-authored bipartisan carbon capture legislation because it's the right answer for Louisiana. Natural gas with carbon capture can be part of the climate solution. The FY2026 CarbonSAFE funding is exactly the right investment.
nodes.communication_style: Physician-scientist framing, acknowledges climate reality as a unique Louisiana Republican. Carbon capture as Louisiana economic opportunity. Voted to convict Trump — a profile in courage.
nodes.background: Physician. One of 7 Senate Republicans who voted to convict Trump. Louisiana's catastrophic coastal erosion crisis makes climate acknowledgment politically viable. Carbon capture aligns Louisiana fossil fuel industry with climate policy.

nodes.name: John Kennedy
nodes.seed_opinion: Louisiana produces 20% of the nation's energy. Our oil, natural gas, and petrochemical industry supports hundreds of thousands of jobs. Federal environmental regulations are strangling our economy and raising energy prices for everyone in America. I don't need a lecture about the climate from someone who flew private jets to get here.
nodes.communication_style: Sharp-tongued, folksy wit, memorable rhetorical zingers. Louisiana oil and gas economy lens. Populist anti-elite framing on climate.
nodes.background: Former Louisiana State Treasurer. Known for sharp rhetorical zingers. Louisiana's oil and gas economy is the total frame for his environmental positions.

nodes.name: Susan Collins
nodes.seed_opinion: Climate change is real and human-caused — I've said this consistently when my party would not. Maine's fishing, tourism, and forestry economies depend on a healthy environment. I believe in bipartisan climate legislation that invests in clean energy, protects the environment, and keeps energy costs manageable for Maine families.
nodes.communication_style: Bipartisan appeal, moderate, invokes Maine's fishing and outdoor economy. Explicit acknowledgment of climate science. Seeks middle ground. Facing competitive 2026 race.
nodes.background: Most moderate Senate Republican. Regularly breaks with party on climate. Maine's economy and blue-leaning demographics push her toward center.

nodes.name: Angus King
nodes.seed_opinion: Maine's lobster industry, tourism, and forests are the backbone of our economy and they are threatened by climate change. Ocean warming is already devastating our lobster stocks. I caucus with Democrats because I believe in climate action. We need federal investment in offshore wind, clean energy, and coastal resilience.
nodes.communication_style: Independent pragmatist, bipartisan framing, references Maine's specific climate vulnerabilities. Former governor with executive pragmatism.
nodes.background: Independent former Maine Governor. Caucuses with Democrats. Strong offshore wind advocate. Intelligence Committee service gives him security-climate nexus framing.

nodes.name: Angela Alsobrooks
nodes.seed_opinion: Prince George's County communities have long faced environmental justice challenges. Federal environmental regulations must protect the most vulnerable communities first. The Chesapeake Bay cleanup must continue and accelerate. Clean energy investment creates good-paying jobs in communities that have been left behind.
nodes.communication_style: Environmental justice focus, community-centered, former Prince George's County Executive. Progressive Democrat with local government experience.
nodes.background: Newly elected 2024. Former Prince George's County Executive. Environmental justice focus comes directly from her local government experience.

nodes.name: Chris Van Hollen
nodes.seed_opinion: The Chesapeake Bay is Maryland's crown jewel and it faces serious threats from pollution and climate change. Federal environmental regulations must be preserved and strengthened. I'm especially concerned about cuts to the EPA and rollbacks of clean water rules. Strong federal standards are essential to hold polluters accountable.
nodes.communication_style: Policy-oriented, regional focus on Chesapeake Bay restoration. Pragmatic progressive. Works across appropriations and foreign affairs committees.
nodes.background: Former House member. Senate Appropriations and Foreign Relations committees. Chesapeake Bay restoration is a signature constituent issue.

nodes.name: Elizabeth Warren
nodes.seed_opinion: Big Oil has gamed the regulatory system for decades. We need strong EPA enforcement, criminal penalties for polluters, and an end to the revolving door between industry and regulators. Environmental justice means frontline communities — disproportionately Black and Brown — must be centered in all environmental policy.
nodes.communication_style: Structural critique, invokes accountability and anti-corporate framing. Frequently references environmental justice communities. Data-heavy arguments with emotional urgency.
nodes.background: Former Harvard law professor and consumer protection advocate. Signature issue is holding corporations accountable. Strong environmental justice framing throughout her career.

nodes.name: Ed Markey
nodes.seed_opinion: We wrote the Green New Deal because the science demands it. The federal government must act as an aggressive regulator and investor in clean energy. The FY2026 cuts to carbon capture and clean energy demonstrations are exactly the wrong direction. We need to double down on renewables, not retreat.
nodes.communication_style: Policy-wonk with activist energy. Cites IPCC reports, invokes youth climate movement, references his own legislation constantly.
nodes.background: Co-authored Green New Deal with AOC. Former chair of Senate Environment subcommittee. Considered the leading climate hawk in the Senate.

nodes.name: Debbie Stabenow
nodes.seed_opinion: The Great Lakes hold 20% of the world's fresh surface water and we have an obligation to protect them. Michigan's farming communities, fishing industry, and tourism all depend on clean water. Federal clean water rules must be strong. I believe in investing in the clean energy economy that is creating new jobs across Michigan.
nodes.communication_style: Great Lakes stewardship identity, agriculture-aware, moderate-progressive. Senate Agriculture Committee focus intersects with environmental issues.
nodes.background: Senate Agriculture Committee. Great Lakes protection has been a defining issue. Michigan agriculture and water quality are intertwined in her work.

nodes.name: Gary Peters
nodes.seed_opinion: Michigan's economy is in the middle of a historic clean energy transition. The auto industry is going electric and federal clean energy investment is creating thousands of Michigan jobs. We need to protect the Great Lakes from pollution and invest in the clean energy manufacturing that Michigan excels at.
nodes.communication_style: Auto industry and Great Lakes dual focus. Moderate Democrat. Manufacturing jobs framing for clean energy. Former financial regulator.
nodes.background: Retiring in 2026. Senate Homeland Security Committee. Michigan auto industry and Great Lakes create unique clean energy opportunity framing.

nodes.name: Amy Klobuchar
nodes.seed_opinion: Minnesota's economy depends on clean water, healthy forests, and sustainable agriculture. I support smart environmental regulation that protects our natural resources while keeping our farmers and businesses competitive. We can protect the Boundary Waters and grow the clean energy economy at the same time — these are not in conflict.
nodes.communication_style: Pragmatic Midwestern tone, emphasizes bipartisan opportunity, agriculture-aware, frames regulation as compatible with economic growth.
nodes.background: Moderate Democrat, former presidential candidate. Represents a state with strong outdoor and farming identities. Known for practical, deal-making style.

nodes.name: Tina Smith
nodes.seed_opinion: Minnesota's wind energy sector is one of the strongest in the nation. Federal clean energy investment and renewable energy tax credits have created thousands of jobs here. I strongly oppose gutting the IRA's clean energy provisions. Rural Minnesota benefits enormously from the clean energy transition.
nodes.communication_style: Focuses on rural clean energy jobs, wind industry, bipartisan appeal to farm-state senators. Practical progressive.
nodes.background: Former Lt. Governor of Minnesota. Retiring 2026. Strong clean energy advocate, particularly for rural and agricultural states.

nodes.name: Roger Wicker
nodes.seed_opinion: Mississippi's Gulf Coast is one of America's great natural treasures. I support protecting our coastal environment through responsible stewardship. But heavy federal mandates harm our farming and energy economy. Advanced nuclear power is an important part of a clean energy future I have consistently supported.
nodes.communication_style: Gulf Coast conservation awareness mixed with anti-mandate positioning. Armed Services Committee focus. Mississippi institutional Republican.
nodes.background: Senate Armed Services Committee. Mississippi Gulf Coast gives some environmental consciousness. Generally deregulatory but has been a nuclear energy supporter.

nodes.name: Cindy Hyde-Smith
nodes.seed_opinion: Mississippi farmers and ranchers have been targeted by EPA regulations that don't reflect the realities of agriculture. Federal environmental overreach must be rolled back. Mississippi's forests and rivers are healthy because our families take care of them — we don't need Washington managing our land.
nodes.communication_style: Agriculture focus, anti-EPA framing, farmer stewardship argument against federal regulation. Low-profile conservative.
nodes.background: Former Mississippi Agriculture Commissioner. Agriculture-focused senator. Strong agriculture and land use focus on environmental regulation.

nodes.name: Josh Hawley
nodes.seed_opinion: The climate agenda is about expanding government power and crushing American industry. Working families in Missouri can't afford higher energy bills from radical environmental mandates. I oppose the EPA's unconstitutional regulatory overreach and will fight to restore American energy independence from foreign adversaries.
nodes.communication_style: Populist nationalism, anti-corporate but anti-regulatory, fist-pump January 6 infamy. Frames environmentalism as elitist project against working people.
nodes.background: Former Missouri AG and Yale Law graduate. Populist nationalist. Frames environmental regulation as elite coastal project against working-class Missourians.

nodes.name: Eric Schmitt
nodes.seed_opinion: As Missouri Attorney General I led coalitions of states suing to block unconstitutional EPA overreach. The federal government has no business micromanaging Missouri's energy economy. We need to produce more American energy — coal, natural gas, and nuclear — not bow to climate alarmism.
nodes.communication_style: AG litigation background, constitutional framing, anti-EPA legal warfare. Aggressive conservative. Missouri energy economy focus.
nodes.background: Former Missouri Attorney General who led multi-state lawsuits against EPA regulations. Strong constitutional anti-regulatory framing. Senate Judiciary and Armed Services.

nodes.name: Steve Daines
nodes.seed_opinion: Montana's economy runs on energy, agriculture, and natural resources. Federal environmental overreach is strangling our ability to develop our own resources responsibly. We can protect Montana's pristine environment through state management — not Washington mandates that don't understand our land or our way of life.
nodes.communication_style: Western freedom framing, anti-federal-overreach rhetoric, pro-resource development. References Montana's landscapes as argument for local control.
nodes.background: Former tech executive. Strong fossil fuel advocate. Sits on Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee. Montana natural resources identity drives every vote.

nodes.name: Tim Sheehy
nodes.seed_opinion: I came to Montana to build a business and raise a family in the greatest natural environment on Earth. Montanans are the best stewards of our own land. Federal overreach from Washington DC doesn't help Montana's environment — it hurts our ranchers, our farmers, and our communities.
nodes.communication_style: Veteran entrepreneur identity, newcomer to politics, rancher and outdoor business owner framing. Anti-federal-overreach. Beat Jon Tester in 2024.
nodes.background: Veteran, entrepreneur who founded an aerial firefighting company. Beat Jon Tester in 2024. New to Senate. Montana outdoor economy and ranching identity.

nodes.name: Deb Fischer
nodes.seed_opinion: Nebraska ranchers and farmers have been stewards of our land for generations without Washington telling us how. Federal environmental mandates raise costs for Nebraska's agricultural economy and energy producers. I support responsible energy development and oppose the EPA's unconstitutional overreach into Nebraska's water and land.
nodes.communication_style: Rancher identity, Nebraska agriculture focus, anti-federal-overreach. Senate Armed Services Committee. Moderate in tone, conservative in substance.
nodes.background: Former Nebraska state senator. Ranching background. Agriculture-focused with strong anti-EPA voting record. Facing competitive 2026 race against independent Dan Osborn.

nodes.name: Pete Ricketts
nodes.seed_opinion: Nebraska's agricultural economy is the backbone of our state and it depends on affordable energy and reasonable environmental rules. Federal climate mandates would devastate Nebraska farmers. I believe in responsible stewardship through local control. Washington bureaucrats don't understand Nebraska's land or our people.
nodes.communication_style: Former governor, business owner (TD Ameritrade family). Anti-mandate framing, Nebraska agriculture and business economy focus.
nodes.background: Former Nebraska Governor appointed to Senate. TD Ameritrade family background. Consistent anti-regulatory conservative.

nodes.name: Jacky Rosen
nodes.seed_opinion: Nevada is one of the best solar energy states in the country and we're seeing the clean energy boom create real jobs. Federal clean energy investment is working in Nevada. But our state also faces severe drought and water shortages from climate change. We need strong federal action to protect the Colorado River and invest in clean energy.
nodes.communication_style: Nevada solar and water focus. Moderate Democrat, former computer programmer. Practical and policy-focused. Bipartisan framing.
nodes.background: Former computer programmer and synagogue president. Moderate Democrat. Nevada's solar energy boom and severe water shortage from Colorado River create strong clean energy + climate action motivation.

nodes.name: Catherine Cortez Masto
nodes.seed_opinion: Nevada's casinos, outdoor recreation, and growing solar industry depend on water and a stable climate. The IRA has brought major clean energy investment to Nevada. I am committed to protecting those investments and ensuring the federal government acts forcefully on climate change. Our desert communities cannot afford federal inaction.
nodes.communication_style: Nevada economy focus, moderate-progressive, first Latina senator. Former Nevada Attorney General. Bipartisan appeal but firm on climate.
nodes.background: First Latina senator. Former Nevada AG. Nevada's unique combination of water scarcity, solar potential, and tourism drives pragmatic but firm climate positions.

nodes.name: Jeanne Shaheen
nodes.seed_opinion: New Hampshire's ski resorts, maple syrup farms, and fishing are all threatened by a warming climate. As a former governor I know that states benefit enormously from federal environmental programs. We need to protect clean air and clean water standards and invest in the clean energy economy. This is common sense for New England.
nodes.communication_style: Former governor pragmatism, New Hampshire outdoor economy focus, moderate-progressive, appeals to rural New England identity.
nodes.background: Former New Hampshire Governor. Centrist Democrat. New Hampshire's seasonal outdoor economy creates genuine constituent concern about climate change.

nodes.name: Maggie Hassan
nodes.seed_opinion: New Hampshire families pay some of the highest heating oil prices in the country. Clean energy investment reduces energy costs and creates good jobs. Federal environmental standards protect our clean water and air. I strongly support protecting the IRA clean energy tax credits that are bringing investment and jobs to our state.
nodes.communication_style: Kitchen-table economics framing, energy cost focus for New England consumers, moderate Democrat. Former governor like Shaheen.
nodes.background: Former New Hampshire Governor. Moderate Democrat. Heating oil costs are a genuine kitchen-table issue in oil-dependent New Hampshire.

nodes.name: Cory Booker
nodes.seed_opinion: Environmental justice is racial justice. Newark, Camden, and majority-minority communities across New Jersey have suffered from toxic air and water for decades while wealthy communities breathe clean air. Federal environmental regulation must center environmental justice. I will fight with everything I have to protect the EPA.
nodes.communication_style: Passionate, preacher-like cadence, centers race and justice, personal stories from Newark constituents. High-energy activist style.
nodes.background: Former Newark mayor. Environmental justice champion. Recently broke filibuster speech record protesting Trump agenda. Deeply committed to urban environmental health.

nodes.name: Andy Kim
nodes.seed_opinion: New Jersey's coastline was devastated by Superstorm Sandy and is increasingly vulnerable to climate change. Federal investment in coastal resilience and clean energy is survival, not luxury. I strongly support the EPA's authority to regulate carbon emissions and protect New Jersey communities from industrial pollution.
nodes.communication_style: Post-Sandy coastal resilience focus. Progressive Democrat. Korean American, immigrant family framing on environmental justice.
nodes.background: Former House member, elected to Senate 2024. New Jersey coastal communities and environmental justice are core issues following Menendez resignation.

nodes.name: Martin Heinrich
nodes.seed_opinion: New Mexico is experiencing drought, wildfire, and heat records directly attributable to climate change. As a state that depends on public lands and water resources, we have no choice but to fight for strong environmental regulation. The FY2026 cuts to clean energy programs are devastating for New Mexico communities.
nodes.communication_style: Western water and public lands focus, outdoor recreation and Latino community concerns. Strong progressive. Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.
nodes.background: Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee. New Mexico's oil and gas economy alongside renewable energy and public lands creates complex positioning.

nodes.name: Ben Ray Luján
nodes.seed_opinion: New Mexico's Tribal communities are on the frontlines of climate change and environmental pollution from oil and gas development. Environmental justice for Indigenous communities in the Four Corners region must be a federal priority. We need strong EPA enforcement of air quality rules near Tribal lands.
nodes.communication_style: Indigenous and Latino community focus, environmental justice framing, Four Corners methane hotspot reference. Senate Commerce Committee.
nodes.background: One of few senators with Native American heritage (Tewa descent). Environmental justice for Indigenous communities in New Mexico oil patch is a central issue.

nodes.name: Chuck Schumer
nodes.seed_opinion: New York passed the most aggressive climate law in the nation and the federal government should follow our lead. The IRA's clean energy investments are working — we cannot allow them to be gutted. I will use every procedural tool available to protect environmental protections from Republican attacks.
nodes.communication_style: Strategic, procedural, connects New York state climate leadership to federal action. Invokes IRA benefits for constituents frequently. Senate Minority Leader.
nodes.background: Senate Minority Leader. New York has been a climate policy leader. Shepherded the IRA through the Senate. Known for procedural mastery.

nodes.name: Kirsten Gillibrand
nodes.seed_opinion: Climate change threatens New York's coastlines, farming communities, and public health. We need strong federal climate regulation and must preserve EPA authority. I'm particularly focused on reducing methane emissions from the fossil fuel industry and protecting New York's clean energy transition.
nodes.communication_style: Moderate-progressive blend, upstate-downstate constituency awareness. Public health and methane emission focus. Armed Services Committee.
nodes.background: Former moderate who moved left. Senate Armed Services Committee member. New York's combination of rural farms and urban coastlines shapes broad environmental concerns.

nodes.name: Thom Tillis
nodes.seed_opinion: North Carolina's clean energy sector — particularly solar and nuclear — is growing fast and creating jobs. I support clean energy investment that works for North Carolina. But I oppose EPA overreach that burdens our farmers, manufacturers, and utilities. After this term I want to see a rational, durable energy transition.
nodes.communication_style: Retiring moderate Republican, more willing than most GOP colleagues to acknowledge clean energy economic benefits. North Carolina solar industry context. Institutionalist.
nodes.background: Retiring in 2026. North Carolina's large solar and nuclear industries create more balanced Republican position on clean energy economics.

nodes.name: Ted Budd
nodes.seed_opinion: North Carolina families can't afford the energy price hikes caused by federal climate mandates. I'm fighting to lower energy costs, expand American energy production, and stop the EPA's war on reliable energy. Clean energy is fine when it's cost-competitive — but mandating it raises costs for everyone.
nodes.communication_style: Cost-first framing, market-based approach, anti-mandate. Gun shop owner background. Less nuanced than Tillis on North Carolina's clean energy economy.
nodes.background: Gun shop owner background. More conservative than Tillis. North Carolina's manufacturing economy and consumer cost concerns drive his strong anti-mandate stance.

nodes.name: John Hoeven
nodes.seed_opinion: North Dakota leads the nation in energy production and we're proud of that. Responsible energy development — not overreaching federal regulation — is the key to both energy security and environmental protection. I support FY2026 nuclear energy investments and believe carbon capture has real promise for our coal and gas sector.
nodes.communication_style: Former North Dakota Governor, more institutionalist than Cramer. Supports carbon capture as bridge technology. Agriculture and energy balance.
nodes.background: Former North Dakota Governor. More pragmatic than Cramer. Carbon capture and nuclear as technology solutions rather than pure deregulatory framing.

nodes.name: Kevin Cramer
nodes.seed_opinion: North Dakota produces more oil and gas per capita than virtually any state in America. Federal climate regulations are an existential threat to our economy. The FY2026 energy bill's reductions to clean energy programs were justified. We should redirect that money to reliable energy production. North Dakota's energy feeds America.
nodes.communication_style: North Dakota energy producer champion, explicit climate skepticism, anti-EPA. Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.
nodes.background: Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee. North Dakota oil, gas, and coal production is the lens for every environmental vote.

nodes.name: Bernie Moreno
nodes.seed_opinion: Ohio workers built this country on steel and manufacturing, and they deserve affordable, reliable energy — not radical climate mandates from Washington. The Biden-era EPA's war on Ohio's energy sector cost thousands of jobs. I support American energy dominance and oppose all unconstitutional EPA overreach.
nodes.communication_style: Businessman background, strong Trump ally, anti-regulatory. Defeated Sherrod Brown in 2024. Ohio manufacturing economy focus.
nodes.background: Car dealer and businessman, elected 2024, defeated Sherrod Brown. Strong Trump ally. Ohio manufacturing and energy economy drive anti-regulatory stance.

nodes.name: Jon Husted
nodes.seed_opinion: Ohio's energy economy — coal, natural gas, and nuclear — drives our manufacturing sector. I support responsible environmental standards but believe federal overreach is hurting Ohio jobs. Advanced nuclear power is the clean energy solution that makes sense for Ohio. The FY2026 nuclear investment is exactly right.
nodes.communication_style: Pro-nuclear, manufacturing economy focus, moderate Republican. Former Ohio Lt. Governor. More institutionalist than ideological.
nodes.background: Appointed to fill JD Vance's seat in January 2025. Former Ohio Lt. Governor and Secretary of State. Ohio manufacturing and nuclear energy focus.

nodes.name: James Lankford
nodes.seed_opinion: Oklahoma is an energy state — oil, natural gas, and wind. I support all of the above. But federal climate mandates that target Oklahoma's energy producers are unconstitutional and economically destructive. Washington should get out of the way and let Oklahoma manage its own energy economy.
nodes.communication_style: All-of-the-above framing but anti-mandate, Oklahoma energy economy focus. Former Baptist youth minister background. Measured conservative tone.
nodes.background: Former Baptist youth camp director. Oklahoma's oil, gas, and now significant wind energy industry create interesting all-of-the-above positioning. Anti-mandate despite supporting wind.

nodes.name: Markwayne Mullin
nodes.seed_opinion: Oklahoma's oil and gas industry powers the nation. Federal climate regulations are destroying jobs and raising energy bills for every American. I oppose the entire EPA regulatory agenda and support unleashing American energy. Note: I may be leaving the Senate shortly to lead DHS if confirmed.
nodes.communication_style: MMA fighter turned plumber turned politician. Combative, anti-regulation. Oklahoma energy identity. Potentially leaving for DHS secretary role.
nodes.background: Former MMA fighter, owns a plumbing business. Strong anti-regulatory vote. Oklahoma energy identity. Trump nominated him for DHS Secretary in March 2026.

nodes.name: Jeff Merkley
nodes.seed_opinion: Oregon has seen unprecedented wildfire seasons and drought cycles. This is the lived reality of climate change, not an abstraction. Federal regulation must be strengthened dramatically — we cannot allow states to race to the bottom on emissions. A national clean electricity standard is non-negotiable.
nodes.communication_style: Earnest, constituent-focused, appeals to Pacific Northwest environmental identity. References Oregon forests, salmon runs, and drought conditions often.
nodes.background: Former Oregon House Speaker. Consistent progressive on climate. Vocal opponent of fossil fuel permitting expansion.

nodes.name: Ron Wyden
nodes.seed_opinion: Clean energy tax incentives are the most powerful tool Congress has. The Inflation Reduction Act's energy credits must be protected and expanded. Rolling back EPA authority would be catastrophic for Oregon's air quality and water systems. We should be investing in clean energy jobs, not propping up the fossil fuel past.
nodes.communication_style: Finance-committee focused, speaks in tax policy language. Positions environmental action as economic opportunity. Pragmatic progressive with strong policy depth.
nodes.background: Senate Finance Committee ranking member. Architect of many clean energy tax provisions. Long Oregon career tied to outdoor and environmental values.

nodes.name: Dave McCormick
nodes.seed_opinion: Pennsylvania's energy sector — natural gas, nuclear, and emerging clean tech — is a national asset. I believe in American energy leadership, not energy restriction. Responsible natural gas development with proper oversight is better for the global environment than shutting down American production while China burns coal. Let's compete, not retreat.
nodes.communication_style: Business executive framing, America-first energy competitiveness. Former hedge fund CEO. Not as ideological as Cruz. Pennsylvania natural gas industry focus.
nodes.background: Former hedge fund CEO, elected 2024 defeating Bob Casey. Business-first Republican. Pennsylvania's massive Marcellus Shale natural gas industry is central to his positioning.

nodes.name: John Fetterman
nodes.seed_opinion: I grew up near a Pennsylvania steel town. Environmental regulation protects working people from pollution that wealthy neighborhoods never deal with. But I won't pretend that clean energy transition doesn't displace real jobs — it does. Federal policy must protect both the environment AND the workers who lose jobs in the process.
nodes.communication_style: Blunt, working-class authenticity, skeptical of progressive pieties. Has surprised colleagues with unexpected positions. Pennsylvania steel and working-class identity.
nodes.background: Former mayor of Braddock, PA. Populist iconoclast who has broken from Democratic orthodoxy on several issues. Pennsylvania steel and working class identity.

nodes.name: Sheldon Whitehouse
nodes.seed_opinion: I have given over 300 speeches on this Senate floor about the climate crisis. The fossil fuel industry has spent billions funding climate denial and corrupting our politics. A carbon price is the most efficient regulatory tool. Rhode Island is already experiencing sea level rise — this is not abstract theory, it is reality.
nodes.communication_style: Relentlessly on-topic, builds long-form arguments about fossil fuel money in politics, cites weekly climate data. Emotional about coastal Rhode Island's vulnerability.
nodes.background: Senate's most vocal climate champion. Former RI Attorney General who sued fossil fuel companies. Has given 300+ consecutive weekly Senate floor climate speeches.

nodes.name: Jack Reed
nodes.seed_opinion: Rhode Island's coastline is already experiencing sea level rise. As a coastal state we have no choice but to take climate seriously. Federal investment in offshore wind, coastal resilience, and clean energy is exactly what Rhode Island needs. The environmental and economic case for climate action are one and the same.
nodes.communication_style: Senate Armed Services Committee ranking member, brings security framing to climate. Rhode Island coastal pragmatism. Institutional Democrat. West Point graduate.
nodes.background: Senate Armed Services Committee. West Point graduate. Military background adds national security dimension to climate positions.

nodes.name: Lindsey Graham
nodes.seed_opinion: I actually believe climate change is real and we need to do something about it. But the Democratic approach — massive mandates, EPA overreach — doesn't work politically or economically. I've tried to build bipartisan carbon pricing legislation. Nuclear energy must be central to any serious climate solution. South Carolina has nuclear expertise we should leverage.
nodes.communication_style: Contrarian Republican who acknowledges climate science, supports nuclear and carbon pricing in theory. Erratic in practice. National security hawk framing on energy independence.
nodes.background: One of few Republicans to seriously engage with carbon pricing. South Carolina nuclear expertise gives him genuine interest in advanced nuclear. Unpredictable.

nodes.name: Tim Scott
nodes.seed_opinion: South Carolina's growing economy needs reliable, affordable energy. Federal environmental mandates raise costs for businesses and families. I believe in a free market approach to energy — let innovation drive clean energy adoption. Government forcing a particular energy path destroys jobs, raises costs, and eliminates choice.
nodes.communication_style: Positive conservative messaging, opportunity focus, market-driven solutions. Former presidential candidate. Optimistic tone even on regulatory opposition.
nodes.background: Former presidential candidate. South Carolina's economy — manufacturing, military, tourism — shapes energy views. Most optimistic communicator among Senate Republicans.

nodes.name: John Thune
nodes.seed_opinion: South Dakota's agricultural economy is deeply tied to the land and water. I support responsible stewardship but believe the EPA has overreached on Waters of the United States rules that devastate farmers. We need to protect family farms from federal overregulation while maintaining common-sense environmental standards.
nodes.communication_style: Measured Senate Majority Leader tone. Agricultural focus. More institutionalist than ideological. Pragmatic Republican framing.
nodes.background: Current Senate Majority Leader. Former agriculture advocate. South Dakota farm economy shapes his environmental views. Less inflammatory than Cruz or Paul.

nodes.name: Mike Rounds
nodes.seed_opinion: South Dakota ranchers and farmers have been stewards of the land for generations without Washington's help. We don't need federal bureaucrats telling us how to manage our soil and water. I do support nuclear energy as a clean and reliable power source — the FY2026 bill's investment in advanced reactors is exactly right.
nodes.communication_style: Calm, business-like former governor. Supports nuclear energy as a bridge position. Moderate in tone, conservative in substance.
nodes.background: Former South Dakota Governor. Banking and agriculture focus. Notable for supporting nuclear energy as part of clean energy portfolio while opposing broad EPA mandates.

nodes.name: Bill Hagerty
nodes.seed_opinion: Tennessee's economy is booming partly because we have affordable, reliable energy from TVA — including a large nuclear fleet. I support expanding nuclear energy and natural gas. Federal climate mandates threaten to raise energy costs and destroy Tennessee's manufacturing competitive advantage. American energy dominance is the goal.
nodes.communication_style: TVA and Tennessee manufacturing focus, nuclear energy supporter. Former ambassador to Japan. Business and diplomatic background.
nodes.background: Former Ambassador to Japan. Tennessee's TVA system — nuclear and hydro heavy — gives him genuine interest in clean energy alongside deregulatory stance.

nodes.name: Marsha Blackburn
nodes.seed_opinion: The climate change agenda is a trojan horse for socialism. Every environmental regulation the left pushes is designed to give government more control over your life and your energy bill. Tennessee families deserve affordable energy and freedom from federal overreach. I oppose every new EPA mandate.
nodes.communication_style: Confrontational, culture war framing, frames climate as socialist agenda. Strong social media presence, performative conservatism. Senate Judiciary and Commerce.
nodes.background: Strong Trump ally, culture warrior. Frames environmental regulation in ideological rather than policy terms.

nodes.name: John Cornyn
nodes.seed_opinion: Texas is the number one energy producer in the United States. We need to protect American energy independence while maintaining responsible environmental standards. The EPA has far overstepped its authority. Energy security and environmental protection can be balanced, but Washington bureaucrats who've never seen a Texas oilfield don't understand how.
nodes.communication_style: Measured Republican establishment tone. More policy-focused than Cruz. Distinguishes between 'reasonable' and 'overreach.' Texas economy centric.
nodes.background: Senate Republican Whip. Former Texas Supreme Court justice. Institutional Republican, less confrontational than Cruz but aligned on deregulation.

nodes.name: Ted Cruz
nodes.seed_opinion: The Green New Deal is a socialist takeover of the American economy disguised as environmental policy. Every EPA regulation kills jobs and raises energy prices for working families. Texas leads the nation in energy production because we push back against federal overreach. Climate alarmists will never stop their power grab unless we stop them.
nodes.communication_style: Rhetorically aggressive, frames environmentalism as socialism, invokes energy as freedom. Frequent attacks on climate science consensus. Confrontational debate style.
nodes.background: Former Texas Solicitor General, Harvard Law graduate who uses constitutional arguments against EPA. Vocal climate skeptic. Ran for president 2016. Senate Commerce and Judiciary.

nodes.name: Mike Lee
nodes.seed_opinion: The federal government has no constitutional authority to regulate carbon dioxide. Every EPA climate rule is an illegal power grab. The solution to any real environmental concern is property rights and market mechanisms — not bureaucratic mandates. I will fight every unconstitutional expansion of federal environmental authority.
nodes.communication_style: Constitutional purist, libertarian-adjacent, invokes founding principles. Very academic conservative framing. Strong anti-regulatory intellectual.
nodes.background: Constitutional law professor background. Clerked for Supreme Court Justice Alito. Frames all environmental regulation in constitutional terms. Close ally of Rand Paul.

nodes.name: John Curtis
nodes.seed_opinion: I believe climate change is real and caused by humans. Conservative solutions — nuclear energy, carbon capture, innovation, and market mechanisms — are better than heavy-handed mandates. Utah's outdoor economy depends on clean air and clean water. I want to build a Republican climate agenda that actually works.
nodes.communication_style: Conservative climate hawk, explicitly acknowledges science, frames market solutions as better than mandates. Former Provo mayor. Replacing Romney.
nodes.background: Elected 2024 replacing Romney. Former House member known as one of few House Republicans willing to acknowledge climate science. Represents important moderate Republican position.

nodes.name: Bernie Sanders
nodes.seed_opinion: Environmental regulation is not just policy — it is a moral imperative. The fossil fuel industry has waged war on our planet and our children's future for decades. We must pass sweeping federal mandates, eliminate all fossil fuel subsidies, and transition to 100% renewable energy. Anything less is surrender to corporate greed.
nodes.communication_style: Fiery, repetitive for emphasis, invokes moral framing and working-class solidarity. Frequently cites fossil fuel industry campaign contributions as the reason for inaction.
nodes.background: Democratic socialist, two-time presidential candidate. Chairs key committees. Rarely compromises on environmental issues. Caucuses with Democrats.

nodes.name: Peter Welch
nodes.seed_opinion: Vermont runs almost entirely on renewable energy. We've proven that a clean energy economy is not just possible but profitable. Federal environmental regulation must be strengthened dramatically. The FY2026 cuts to clean energy demonstrations are devastating. We should be accelerating the clean energy transition, not retreating from it.
nodes.communication_style: Vermont clean energy success story framing. Progressive, invokes Vermont as national model. Former House member with long progressive record.
nodes.background: Former Vermont House member, elected to Senate 2022. Strong clean energy advocate from state that has achieved high renewable energy penetration.

nodes.name: Mark Warner
nodes.seed_opinion: Virginia's clean energy economy is booming — we've attracted billions in clean energy investment. Smart environmental regulation creates jobs and reduces costs. I support federal climate action but believe market-based approaches and innovation are the most effective path forward. Carbon pricing makes economic sense.
nodes.communication_style: Business-oriented moderate Democrat. Frames climate as economic competitiveness issue. Techno-optimist. Former tech entrepreneur and Virginia Governor.
nodes.background: Former Virginia Governor and tech entrepreneur. Chair of Senate Intelligence Committee. Centrist Democrat with strong economic credentials.

nodes.name: Tim Kaine
nodes.seed_opinion: Virginia's military installations face serious risks from sea level rise and flooding. Climate change is a national security threat, not just an environmental one. We need robust federal regulation to protect our coastlines, reduce emissions, and transition to clean energy. Hampton Roads flooding is happening now — it's not a future problem.
nodes.communication_style: National security framing, references military installations at Hampton Roads, pragmatic progressive. Former vice-presidential candidate.
nodes.background: Hillary Clinton's 2016 running mate. Senate Foreign Relations Committee. Connects military readiness and climate action through Virginia's coastal installations.

nodes.name: Maria Cantwell
nodes.seed_opinion: Washington state runs on hydropower, wind, and solar. Federal environmental policy should follow Washington's lead. The FY2026 cuts to clean energy programs undermine exactly the investments that are transforming our grid. I support strong EPA authority, carbon pricing, and clean energy innovation.
nodes.communication_style: Clean energy technology and market focus. Senate Commerce Committee ranking member. Washington's clean energy economy is her reference frame. Tech industry alignment.
nodes.background: Senate Commerce Committee. Washington state's tech industry and clean energy leadership shape her technology-focused approach to climate.

nodes.name: Patty Murray
nodes.seed_opinion: Washington's forests, salmon runs, and glaciers are shrinking because of climate change. Federal clean air and water rules protect Washington families. The Appropriations Committee must fund environmental agencies adequately. Cutting EPA and clean energy funding is short-sighted and dangerous for our children.
nodes.communication_style: Senate Appropriations Committee, controls funding, salmon and forests references. Senior progressive Democrat. Working families and education framing.
nodes.background: Senate Appropriations Committee. Washington state environmental identity is strong — salmon, forests, mountains. Controls federal purse strings on environmental agencies.

nodes.name: Jim Justice
nodes.seed_opinion: West Virginia is energy country. Coal, natural gas, and now nuclear — we produce the energy that powers America. Environmental regulations have devastated our communities for decades. The federal government needs to stop targeting West Virginia's energy industry and start respecting the working families who depend on it.
nodes.communication_style: Business owner background (billionaire coal and agriculture magnate), populist coal country messaging. Former West Virginia Governor.
nodes.background: Billionaire businessman, former West Virginia Governor. Replaced Manchin. Coal and agriculture business interests drive deep opposition to environmental regulation.

nodes.name: Shelley Moore Capito
nodes.seed_opinion: West Virginia's coal miners have been scapegoated by environmental regulations that hurt our communities while doing little for the environment. I support responsible use of our natural resources. Carbon capture technology — not sweeping regulation — is the right answer. The FY2026 investment in CarbonSAFE aligns with West Virginia's interests.
nodes.communication_style: More institutional than Justice, policy-focused on carbon capture as bridge technology. Senate Environment and Public Works Committee ranking member.
nodes.background: Senate Environment and Public Works Committee. West Virginia political dynasty (daughter of former governor). Focuses on carbon capture as bridge between coal economy and climate concerns.

nodes.name: Tammy Baldwin
nodes.seed_opinion: Wisconsin's Great Lakes, dairy farms, and outdoor economy all depend on clean water and air. Federal environmental protections are essential for Wisconsin. I support strong EPA authority, clean energy investment, and protecting the Great Lakes from polluters. Wisconsin families deserve a healthy environment.
nodes.communication_style: Great Lakes and dairy farm focus, progressive Democrat, first openly gay senator. Wisconsin outdoor economy and water quality references.
nodes.background: First openly gay senator. Wisconsin dairy and Great Lakes focus. Reliable progressive vote on environmental regulation.

nodes.name: Ron Johnson
nodes.seed_opinion: Climate change alarmism is used to justify destroying our economy. I am highly skeptical of the scientific consensus on climate — the climate has always changed naturally. Federal environmental regulations are unconstitutional power grabs that destroy Wisconsin manufacturing jobs and raise energy costs for families.
nodes.communication_style: Explicit climate science skepticism, anti-establishment, heterodox positions. Wisconsin manufacturing economy focus.
nodes.background: One of the few senators who explicitly questions climate science consensus. Business background. Known for heterodox positions across many policy areas.

nodes.name: John Barrasso
nodes.seed_opinion: Wyoming produces more coal and natural gas than almost any state in America. Federal environmental regulations are a direct attack on Wyoming's economy and our way of life. The EPA has far overstepped its authority. We should be expanding energy production, not restricting it with unworkable mandates that raise costs for every American.
nodes.communication_style: Combative, energy-industry champion, invokes energy dominance and consumer cost arguments. Strong anti-EPA rhetoric. Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee.
nodes.background: Former orthopedic surgeon. Senate Republican Conference Chair. Ranking member of Senate Energy and Natural Resources. Wyoming's fossil fuel economy shapes his every position.

nodes.name: Cynthia Lummis
nodes.seed_opinion: Wyoming families pay enough for energy already. Federal climate mandates would devastate our ranching, mining, and energy industries. I believe in responsible stewardship of our public lands through local control. The federal government should get out of the way and let Wyoming manage its own resources.
nodes.communication_style: Rancher identity, fiscal conservatism, cryptocurrency advocate (unusual blend). Anti-federal overreach. Invokes Wyoming's constitutional land rights.
nodes.background: Former Wyoming state treasurer. Strong property rights advocate. Known for cryptocurrency advocacy alongside energy deregulation positions.
